title: Forward Strategy: Next-Generation Energy, National Digital Infrastructure, and Non-Traditional Threats

statistics.context: ITER is the world's largest fusion experiment, a collaboration of 35 nations under construction in southern France. Originally estimated at EUR 5 billion, costs have escalated to approximately USD 22 billion. First plasma has been delayed to the early 2030s. ITER aims to demonstrate net energy gain (Q≥10) from a magnetically confined deuterium-tritium plasma.
statistics.verification_note: ITER Organization official cost and schedule updates; confirmed by ITER Council reports and GAO assessments

statistics.context: The Fusion Industry Association's 2024 survey reports over USD 6.2 billion in cumulative private investment across 43+ fusion companies worldwide. Major recipients include Commonwealth Fusion Systems (~$2B), Helion Energy (~$577M+), TAE Technologies (~$1.2B), and Tokamak Energy. Investment accelerated significantly after the NIF ignition milestone in December 2022.
statistics.verification_note: Fusion Industry Association annual survey; cross-referenced with Crunchbase and PitchBook funding data

statistics.metric: NIF fusion ignition net energy gain factor
statistics.unit: factor (energy output / laser energy input to target)
statistics.context: On December 5, 2022, the National Ignition Facility achieved fusion ignition for the first time in a laboratory setting. The experiment delivered 2.05 MJ of laser energy to a deuterium-tritium target and produced approximately 3.15 MJ of fusion energy output — a gain factor of ~1.5. This was a scientific milestone; the total facility energy consumption (~300 MJ) far exceeded the output, so commercial viability requires a fundamentally different approach.
statistics.verification_note: Published in Physical Review Letters; confirmed by DOE and LLNL press releases

statistics.metric: Number of private fusion companies globally
statistics.context: The fusion industry has grown from fewer than 10 private companies in 2015 to over 43 in 2024, pursuing diverse approaches including tokamaks, stellarators, field-reversed configurations, inertial confinement, and magnetized target fusion. This private sector surge complements government programs and accelerates the timeline toward commercial fusion.
statistics.verification_note: Fusion Industry Association member directory and annual survey

statistics.context: The NorNed cable between Norway and the Netherlands is 580 km long, transmitting 700 MW at ±450 kV DC. It has been operational since 2008. Even longer HVDC projects are under development, including the North Sea Link (Norway-UK, 720 km, operational 2021) and proposed Australia-Singapore Sun Cable (~4,200 km). These demonstrate the technical feasibility of long-distance submarine power transmission relevant to a potential Taiwan-Japan or Taiwan-Philippines interconnection.
statistics.verification_note: TenneT and Statnett public documentation; NorNed operational data

statistics.metric: Taiwan-Japan shortest submarine distance (Yonaguni to Taiwan)

source.name: Geographic measurement (Yonaguni Island to northeastern Taiwan coast)

statistics.context: Yonaguni Island (Japan's westernmost territory) is approximately 111 km from Taiwan's northeastern coast, making a Taiwan-Japan HVDC submarine cable interconnection technically straightforward by modern standards. Existing HVDC cables (NorNed 580 km, North Sea Link 720 km) far exceed this distance. A 1-2 GW interconnection could provide mutual emergency power backup and enhance both nations' energy resilience.
statistics.verification_note: Geographic distance calculation; consistent with maritime charts

statistics.context: Estonia's e-Residency program has enrolled over 110,000 digital residents from 180+ countries since launch in 2014. Estonia's X-Road platform underpins 99% of government services digitally, and the country's KSI Blockchain secures government data integrity. This model demonstrates a small nation leveraging digital sovereignty as a strategic asset — directly relevant to Taiwan's positioning.
statistics.verification_note: e-Estonia official statistics; e-Residency dashboard public data

statistics.unit: EU member states plus Norway and Liechtenstein
statistics.context: The European Blockchain Services Infrastructure (EBSI) involves all 27 EU member states plus Norway and Liechtenstein (29 total). EBSI provides cross-border public services including verifiable credentials, document traceability, and trusted data sharing. The multi-national architecture demonstrates how sovereign blockchain can facilitate international interoperability — a model for Taiwan's regional integration strategy.
statistics.verification_note: European Commission EBSI official documentation and European Blockchain Partnership membership list

statistics.metric: AARO UAP reports received (cumulative through FY2023)
statistics.context: AARO's 2024 annual report to Congress documented 801 cumulative UAP reports (510 from prior years plus 291 new reports in FY2023). Of these, a significant portion remain unresolved after initial analysis. AARO categorizes resolved cases into airborne clutter, natural atmospheric phenomena, USG or industry programs, and foreign adversary systems. The institutionalization of UAP reporting and investigation reflects a paradigm shift toward treating anomalous aerial phenomena as legitimate national security and airspace sovereignty concerns.
statistics.verification_note: AARO Annual Report to Congress FY2023; publicly available via AARO.mil

statistics.metric: Countries with formal government UAP investigation programs

source.name: NASA UAP Independent Study Team Report / AARO

statistics.context: At least six countries maintain formal government-level UAP investigation programs: the United States (AARO under DoD/DNI), France (GEIPAN/CAIPAN under CNES), the United Kingdom (MoD historical program, renewed interest), Canada (historical and informal ongoing), Brazil (official Air Force program), and Japan (MoD establishing investigation capacity). Several other countries including Australia and Argentina maintain less formal monitoring. The international institutionalization of UAP investigation provides a framework for Taiwan to participate without bearing disproportionate political risk.
statistics.verification_note: Cross-referenced from NASA UAP study, AARO reports, CNES/GEIPAN public documentation, and Japan MoD announcements

statistics.metric: NASA UAP Independent Study Team members
statistics.context: NASA convened a 16-member independent study team of scientists, engineers, and aerospace experts to examine how NASA could contribute to UAP research using scientific methodology. The team's September 2023 report recommended NASA take a more active role in UAP data collection and analysis, leading to the appointment of a NASA Director of UAP Research. This approach of embedding UAP investigation within a respected civilian scientific institution provides a model for Taiwan.
statistics.verification_note: NASA UAP Independent Study Team Report, September 14, 2023; publicly available

statistics.metric: JAXA Space-Based Solar Power target demonstration
statistics.context: JAXA has been developing space-based solar power (SBSP) technology since the 2000s. Their roadmap targets a ground-based wireless power transmission demonstration and aims for a 1 GW commercial SBSP system. In 2024, JAXA successfully demonstrated microwave power transmission over a distance of approximately 50 meters. While commercial deployment remains decades away, SBSP represents a theoretically blockade-immune energy source for island nations like Japan and Taiwan.
statistics.verification_note: JAXA SBSP research program documentation; ground demonstration results reported in Japanese media and JAXA press releases
